words.definition: A named container that stores a value which can change while a program runs.
A reusable block of code that performs a specific task.
A structure that repeats a block of code multiple times.
An ordered collection that stores multiple values in a single variable.
A statement that runs code only when a given test is true.
Data that a program receives, often from the user.
Data that a program produces and shows or returns.
A sequence of characters used to represent text.
A whole number without a decimal point.
A value that can only be true or false.
A structure where the last item added is the first one removed (LIFO).
A structure where the first item added is the first one removed (FIFO).
A hierarchical structure made of nodes connected like branches.
A structure of nodes connected by edges to show relationships.
A chain of nodes where each node points to the next one.
A single element in a data structure that holds data and links.
A tree-based structure used to quickly find the largest or smallest value.
A function that turns data into a fixed-size value for fast lookup.
An ordered collection that stores multiple values in a single variable.
A structure that links each unique key to a value.
A device that directs data between different computer networks.
A small unit of data sent across a network.
A set of rules that controls how data is sent and received.
A security system that filters incoming and outgoing network traffic.
A computer that provides data or services to other computers.
A computer or app that requests services from a server.
An endpoint that lets two programs communicate over a network.
A human-readable name that identifies a website on the internet.
A device that connects computers within the same network.
The delay before data starts transferring across a network.
A collection of related data organized into rows and columns.
A request used to read or change data in a database.
The structure that defines how a database is organized.
A structure that speeds up searching for data in a table.
A connection between two tables in a database.
A column whose value uniquely identifies each row in a table.
A column that links one table to the primary key of another.
A group of database operations treated as a single unit.
An organized collection of data stored for easy access.
A single row of related data in a table.
The process of scrambling data so only authorized people can read it.
Harmful software designed to damage or gain access to a system.
A scam that tricks people into revealing private information.
A security system that filters incoming and outgoing network traffic.
A weakness in a system that an attacker can exploit.
A technique or code that takes advantage of a vulnerability.
A secret string used to verify a user's identity.
The process of confirming that someone is who they claim to be.
A possible danger that could harm a system or its data.
An attempt to damage, steal from, or disrupt a system.
